results.title: 6 Best Wishlist Apps for Couples in 2026 (Tested and ... - Farha
results.content:
What sets Farha apart is its language support: English, French, and Arabic with full right-to-left layout. No other wishlist app in this comparison supports Arabic. For couples where one partner's first language is Arabic — common in France, Belgium, Canada, and across the MENA region — this removes a real friction point.

### Pros

 Add items from any online store, not just one retailer
 Secret reservations: reserved items are hidden from the wishlist owner
 Group gifting lets friends chip in on big-ticket items for your partner
 Shared links work in any browser, no account required for gift givers
 Supports English, French, and Arabic (only app with Arabic + RTL)
 Price drop alerts — know when a wishlist item goes on sale
 100% free, no premium tier

### Cons [...] ### Cons

 Newer app with a growing community
 No Safari browser extension yet (coming soon)

### Pricing

Free for all features. No paywalls on sharing, reservations, or group gifting.

Read more: How group gifting works

## 2. CoupleWish, best purpose-built for couples

CoupleWish is a new app built specifically around the couples gifting problem. It launched in beta in 2025 with a core feature called the "secret reservation" — when one partner marks something as a planned gift, the item is hidden from the other partner's view. The concept is solid and directly addresses the surprise-preservation problem. [...] The app is free in its current beta. The product catalog is more limited than universal apps, and there's no confirmation that it supports non-US stores. It's English-only, which is a constraint for international couples. Still, for English-speaking couples who want a purpose-built experience and don't need multilingual support, it's worth testing.

### Pros

 Built entirely around the couples gifting use case
 Secret reservation feature is the core product, not an afterthought
 Clean, simple interface
 Free in beta

### Cons

 English only — no multilingual support
 Early-stage product with a small user base
 Unclear support for international or non-US stores
 Limited universal adding compared to established apps
 No group gifting feature for friends contributing to couple occasions

results.title: What do you think is the best wishlist app? : r/Gifts
results.content:
As Christmas is coming up, I have been looking into different apps and websites that are not only easy for me to use, but also easy for my family members to use when either making their own lists or just accessing my list. I have been using GoWish and I do honestly love it because it also has a website but my biggest problem is that it requires anyone that wants to view your list to create their own account which can be pretty annoying when you are only trying to view a list for one occasion. I have also used Amazon which is convenient for most as a lot of people use it but you can only link things from amazon, so if there are items from some random clothing website then you cant add it. That is my biggest complaint from amazon. I have also tried Notion and Google docs but those are kinds [...] have also tried Notion and Google docs but those are kinds of wishlist that you have to do every single thing yourself. Which just becomes annoying especially when transferring all of your things when first getting the app. Are their any wishlist apps that you know of that also have website integration, don't require you to sign up or download an app to view the list, and has features like link integration, image attachments, and list filters? I'm looking for wishlists in general, not just Christmas but I thought this would be the best place to post! I also find it helpful when the app allows you to share the link directly from the website to then upload to the app. I use IOS but I think it would be helpful to hear Android options as well! [...] # What do you think is the best wishlist app?

results.title: Farha vs. WishApp (2026 Comparison)
results.content:
### Gifting and group features

Farha and WishApp split this one. Farha has two things WishApp doesn't: group gifting, where several people pool money toward one big item, and thank-you notes you send after the gift arrives. Both are thoughtful. WishApp goes wider instead, with collaborative lists where a couple can co-manage one wishlist, a follow system, a discover feed, and full reserved-and-received tracking with a note like 'from Sarah'. Both name Secret Santa as an occasion, but neither draws names automatically yet.

### Ease of use and trust [...] ### Sharing, privacy and reservations

Both apps get the core right. Share a list with one link, and friends open it in a browser with no account needed. Both hide reservations so the owner never sees who claimed what, which is the whole point of a gift list. Farha adds a nice touch here: it supports Arabic with full right-to-left layout, plus French, which very few wishlist apps bother with. WishApp answers with QR codes, all currencies, and a wider language reach on web, but no Arabic. For a MENA or francophone family, Farha is genuinely the friendlier option.

### Gifting and group features [...] Skip to content

WishApp LogoWishApp Logo

# Farha vs. WishApp (2026 Comparison)

Farha compared with WishApp, two wishlist apps side by side

Farha is a brand-new wishlist app from a solo founder, built around occasions and a few features WishApp doesn't have. Here is how the two stack up on price, the devices you can use, and the gifting tools that decide which one sticks.

Last updated: June 2026

Looking for a Farha alternative? Farha is a free, very new app with two real standouts: 24/7 price drop alerts and group gifting that WishApp doesn't match. But it's iOS-only today, with no Android and no browser extension. WishApp runs everywhere and is proven. Among Farha alternatives, WishApp is the one we'd pick.

## Feature comparison
